reason: The node omits a critical hypothesis: it states 'Under m affine constraints Tr(a_j rho) = beta_j' but never specifies that rho must be an EXTREME POINT of the constrained set C = {rho in S(M_n(C)) : Tr(a_j rho) = beta_j}. The multiplicity bound Sigma n_j^2 <= m+1 (from Node 1.4 via Node 1.3) applies ONLY when phi is an extreme point of C. Without this hypothesis, the bound does not hold -- any density matrix rho of any rank satisfying the constraints would trivially exist. The extremality condition is the essential premise that makes the rank bound non-trivial.
